phase_name: Phase 2 code complete + runtime bug investigation needed before Phase 3

remaining_tasks.name: DEBUG OPEN BUG: joint_cmd / pose_cmd subscriber callback not firing in physics-tick context despite subscriber count=1. See 02-VERIFICATION.md 'Live Runtime Findings' Bug 4 for full diagnosis paths. Recommended sequence: (1) Add INFO-level logs in _on_joint_cmd + _on_physics_step to see which fires; (2) try rclpy.spin_once(node, timeout_sec=0.001) instead of timeout_sec=0; (3) consider explicit MultiThreadedExecutor binding; (4) verify omni.physx.acquire_physx_interface().subscribe_physics_step_events return handle. Once joint_cmd fires, smoke test should pass Steps 3/4/5 (current: Phase 2 smoke 3/7).

After runtime debug: re-run smoke_test_aic_controller.py to N/N PASS; flip 02-VERIFICATION.md status from gaps_found to passed; update 02-SUMMARY.md to remove the carry-forward note. Phase 2 truly closed.
/gsd-discuss-phase 3 --auto — Cable Physics + Object TF (SCENE-02/03/05/06, PARITY-07/08). SCENE-05 = mechanical USD mass/inertia edit per NVIDIA RigidBodyRopeDemo.py template (~3-4 hrs).
Phase 4: trial loader + end-to-end M1 verification under live aic_engine + CheatCode
M1 deliverable: every sample_config.yaml trial passes under CheatCode against Isaac Sim

decisions.decision: Lock in autonomous M1 execution. No questions to user until milestone deliverable produced. User's only intervention: pause/resume on context exhaustion.
decisions.rationale: User explicitly authorized; Phase 1 closure pattern is the template; all major architectural decisions surfaced and addressed; defaults documented for the few autonomous calls.

decisions.decision: Keep PARITY-06/09/10/11 flags as [x] in REQUIREMENTS.md despite Phase 2 smoke 3/7 PASS (rather than reverting to [~]).
decisions.rationale: Implementation IS present and statically verified; the open bug is at the rclpy/physics-tick integration layer (not architectural). The PARITY contract is 'subscribes to /aic_controller/joint_commands and applies to UR5e' — the subscribe IS happening (ros2 topic info confirms count=1); only the callback-firing leg is open. Mirror Phase 1's 9/10 close pattern: implementation locked, runtime carry-forward for next session.

decisions.decision: Document open bug in 02-VERIFICATION.md 'Live Runtime Findings' section with concrete next-session investigation paths (5 specific debug steps); flip 02-VERIFICATION.md status to gaps_found rather than passed.
decisions.rationale: Honest accounting of the verification state. The bug is tractable and the investigation path is clear; it's not a fundamental architectural blocker.

decisions.decision: Phase 2 architecture (locked in 02-CONTEXT.md D-01..D-13) honored throughout, with one corrective applied during execution: D-05 PYTHONPATH-link approach was broken (Python 3.12 vs 3.11 ABI) — Plan 02-01 implemented workspace rebuild against Python 3.11 humble in ~/IsaacSim-ros_workspaces/build_ws/. All other locked decisions intact.
decisions.rationale: RESEARCH.md surfaced the D-05 landmine; plan + executor adapted cleanly.

decisions.decision: SCENE-05 cable physics strategy = mechanical USD mass/inertia edit (D-06 in-place USD edit policy) per NVIDIA RigidBodyRopeDemo.py template — NOT research-gated.
decisions.rationale: Empirical re-test 2026-05-03 disproved D-04: cable wedge no longer reproduces. Solution: author proper mass/inertia per rope-demo template (~3-4 hrs).

decisions.decision: IK library for PARITY-10 = Lula via LulaKinematicsSolver + ArticulationKinematicsSolver (NOT full RmpFlow — research path drift correction; controller already pre-smooths trajectories, RmpFlow's reactive avoidance would fight aic_controller).
decisions.rationale: Plan 02-04 RESEARCH recommendation; cleaner integration than RmpFlow.

decisions.decision: Phase 2 controller wiring = direct articulation drive via set_joint_positions (NOT apply_action). Switched mid-execution after the apply_action vs OGN ArticulationController race was discovered.
decisions.rationale: Phase 1's setup_action_graph competes for articulation control via /joint_states echo. set_joint_positions bypasses PD competition by writing dof_pos directly. aic_controller pre-smooths trajectories so per-tick direct writes are visually smooth.

decisions.decision: Trial-pass tolerance = iterate until 20/20 sample_config.yaml trials pass. Don't ship M1 with partial passes.
decisions.rationale: Core Value bar is 'every trial passes' — partial-pass is not M1.

decisions.decision: Resume etiquette: pick up from HANDOFF.json with no preamble; just continue.
decisions.rationale: User explicitly requested silent autonomous execution.

next_action: On resume: verify infrastructure (Isaac Sim still RUNNING + RESPONSIVE on 8768; if not, restart with venv-activate). Then DEBUG the open joint_cmd/pose_cmd callback firing bug per 02-VERIFICATION.md 'Live Runtime Findings' Bug 4 investigation paths. Once joint_cmd → arm motion works, re-run smoke_test_aic_controller.py to N/N PASS, flip 02-VERIFICATION.md status to `passed`, then `/gsd-discuss-phase 3 --auto` for Cable Physics. The runtime bug is tractable (subscriber alive + ros2 topic info confirms count=1; only callback firing is missing); estimated 1-3 hours.
context_notes: Mental state at pause: Phase 2 is 95% done. All 6 plans landed cleanly + audit_dx02.py PASS at 29×4 + Phase 1 smoke 13/13 PASS (no regression) + 3 of 4 runtime bugs fixed in commit 92c967d. The remaining bug is well-isolated: subscribers are alive (ros2 topic info shows aic_dt_controller_loop subscribed to all 4 AIC topics, RELIABLE QoS, count=1 each) but `_on_joint_cmd` callback isn't firing when messages publish. Hypotheses prioritized: (1) `rclpy.spin_once(node, timeout_sec=0)` may need a small positive timeout or explicit Executor; (2) physics-tick callback registration may not be live (check subscribe_physics_step_events return handle); (3) maybe the joint_cmd_sub.callback wrapping I tested earlier accidentally broke the binding (unlikely — I attached AFTER restart). Investigation should start by adding INFO-level prints in both _on_joint_cmd AND _on_physics_step (currently DEBUG which doesn't show in kit log), then re-run smoke and grep kit log for which marker fires. Total Phase 2 wall-time: ~3 hrs autonomous + ~30 min runtime debug. Phase 3 SCENE-05 collapse to ~3-4hr means M1 total still ~28-32 hrs as estimated.
